subject: the person running the skill, in their own recorded conversations

scale.na: n/a — insufficient signal. Used whenever nothing quotable supports a score; never guess a number.

rules.evidence: Every score cites >=1 verbatim transcript quote with timestamp. Scores of 3-4 on decision fidelity may rest on the moment's framing quotes plus a register check showing no relevant Active decision was contradicted.
rules.fairness_pass: Before finalizing, run a skeptic over every 0-1: was the decision Active in the register at the moment's date? Is the 'claim asserted as fact' actually a Validated assumption? Does the quote carry the reading? A refutation that stands raises the score.
rules.attribution: Diarization check per _shared/decision-guardrails.md section 3. Uncertain attribution is noted on every finding from that transcript and softens verdict language.
rules.scope: Internal team conversations only. External calls (evidence interviews, customers, investors, prospects) are never scored under this rubric.

dimensions.name: Decision fidelity
dimensions.question: Do you honor settled decisions, or re-litigate them without new evidence?
reads: decisions register: fetch unfiltered, check Type (untyped-record rule), then Type=Decision, Status=Active, scoped to the moment's Area (decision-guardrails section 7)
per matched decision: Reversibility (section 8), Agreed by, Decided date

anchors.0: Evidence-free re-litigation of an Active one-way door; being in that decision's Agreed by keeps it at 0 even with hedges.
anchors.1: Evidence-free re-litigation of an Active decision (two-way door, or you were not a party to it).
anchors.2: The moment overlaps a settled decision's ground and ignores it — not contradicted, just unacknowledged.
anchors.3: Respects settled decisions and acknowledges the directly relevant Active ones — including reopening a one-way door with genuine new evidence, the DEC id named and the irreversibility acknowledged.
anchors.4: No directly relevant settled decision is touched, or you explicitly reopen a two-way door citing the DEC id and the new evidence that changes it. Reopening well scores top, by design.

dimensions.name: Assumption transparency
dimensions.question: Are your load-bearing claims named as assumptions, or asserted as facts?
reads: assumptions register: Title, Description, Status, Confidence

anchors.0: Load-bearing untested claims asserted as facts.
anchors.1: Claims hedged ('I think', 'probably') but never named as assumptions.
anchors.2: Some claims flagged as assumptions; none mapped to the register or proposed as new records.
anchors.3: Load-bearing claims explicitly flagged; the key ones mapped to ASM ids or proposed as new.
anchors.4: Every load-bearing claim either evidence-backed (says what evidence) or named as an assumption with an honest confidence read, mapped or proposed for the register.

dimensions.name: Experiment-first thinking
dimensions.question: Do you propose the cheapest honest test before asking for commitment?
dimensions.scored_when: pitch moments (there is an ask). Claims-only moments default to n/a; a voluntarily proposed test in a claims moment is recorded as a credit note alongside the n/a.

anchors.0: Asks for full commitment with no test; opinion treated as sufficient.
anchors.1: Gestures at 'we should validate' with no concrete test or bar.
anchors.2: Proposes a test, but an expensive high-rung one where a cheaper rung could honestly falsify.
anchors.3: Proposes a concrete falsifiable test at a sensible rung before the ask.
anchors.4: Proposes the cheapest rung that can honestly move the belief, states a pass/kill bar, and stakes the ask on the outcome.

dimensions.name: Language concreteness
dimensions.question: Concrete and canonical, or abstract and undefined?
reads: terminology check: _shared/ubiquitous-language.md, audience Internal — a hit counts only when the usage actually conflicts with the Avoid bullet's rationale, not on any occurrence of the banned string
abstraction scan: load-bearing nouns with no glossary record and no in-moment definition, weighed by the weight they carry

anchors.0: Built on undefined abstractions ('platform play', 'synergy') and/or genuine banned-phrasing misuse.
anchors.1: Mostly abstract; few numbers or named specifics; term misuse.
anchors.2: Core claim concrete, supporting argument abstract, or several load-bearing unknown terms.
anchors.3: Concrete (numbers, named customers, specific behaviours); canonical terms used correctly.
anchors.4: Concrete throughout; every abstraction glossary-backed or defined in the moment itself.

per_moment: the Read — verdict first, evidence after
report.improve_next: For every dimension scored <=2 this run: one concrete, checkable action for the next conversation (e.g. 'name the DEC id before proposing anything near it', 'state the kill bar out loud'). Max 3 actions per run — pick the highest-leverage.
report.trends: Dimension score sequences across runs (n/a kept in place), the re-litigation ledger (repeated DEC-id citations), standing patterns once seen in >=2 runs.
